definitions.text: Either of two people (or, less commonly, animals) who shared the same uterus at the same time; one who was born at the same birth as a sibling.
Either of two similar or closely related objects, entities etc.
A room in a hotel, guesthouse, etc. with two beds; a twin room.
To split, part; to go away, depart.
To join, unite; to form links between (now especially of two places in different countries); to pair with.
examples: Reading, the English town, is twinned with Clonmel in Ireland.
Coventry twinned with Dresden as an act of peace and reconciliation, both cities having been heavily bombed during the war.

definitions.text: Double; dual; occurring as a matching pair.
